subject: Statistics
title: Aging Curves in Sports
description: A 60-second lesson on Aging Curves in Sports.

hook.text: At what age do athletes peak? Baseball players peak at 27. Sprinters at 25. Golfers at 34.

buildup.text: Aging curves plot performance against age across thousands of careers to find the typical peak.

discovery.text: The decline after the peak is usually faster than the rise before it. The downslope is steep.

twist.text: Survivorship bias distorts aging curves. Only good players last to age 35 — making late-career stats look better.

climax.text: Teams use aging curves to decide contract length. Paying a 32-year-old for 7 years is statistically risky.

punchline.text: Every athlete declines. The data shows when and how fast.

quiz.question: How does survivorship bias affect aging curves in sports?
options: Only good players last to older ages, making late-career average performance look better than it really is
It makes young players look worse than they are
It has no effect on aging curves
